Empires & Puzzles Frond Review: The Anti-Boost Mantis Assassin

Is Frond still the king of boosted health destruction? We break down his massive 900 extra damage mechanic, his post-buff stats, and why this 'Mantis' might just be the most dangerous insect in your roster against modern meta teams.
Frond Review: The Forest's Answer to Boosted Health
Frond: The Mantis That Feasts on Boosted Health

family, originally released in the Covenant Summon portal in late 2023. While he might look like a robotic praying mantis who got lost on the way to a gardening convention, his kit is hyper-specialized for one thing: punishing enemies who dare to exceed their maximum HP.

In an era dominated by overhealers like Hathor, Arco, and the endless parade of boosted health supports, Frond isn't just a damage dealer; he's a hard counter. If you see a grey health bar, Frond sees a free meal.

Frond boasts one of the more unique designs in the game—a sleek, technological interpretation of a forest guardian. He resembles a biomechanical mantis or an elven construct, with glowing green energy coursing through a wooden and metallic frame. It’s a refreshing departure from the usual "angry guy with axe" aesthetic. The community has affectionately dubbed him "The Mantis," "The Green Bug," or occasionally "Rudolph's Cousin" due to the vaguely antler-like structures on his head. He definitely looks like he’s judging your lack of recycling.

This is where the gap is most felt. Compared to the 1300 Def / 2200 HP behemoths released recently, Frond is significantly squishier.
He is a "Glass Cannon" by modern standards. He hits hard, but he cannot take a punch like the newer Construct or Astral heroes. You

Special Skill: Fury of the Ancient Forest
Frond has received significant buffs since his release, and his current numbers are terrifying if the conditions are met.
This is a solid baseline. Even without the bonus, 375% to three targets at Fast speed is competitive with many modern snipers.
This is the headline act. If the target has
boosted health, they take an additional
target hit. If you hit three enemies with boosted health, that's an extra 2700 damage across the board, on top of the 375% hit. This virtually guarantees a kill on overhealed targets.

This secondary effect is often overlooked but is incredibly potent against teams that constantly regenerate boosted health (looking at you, Toon Vivica and Hathor). It turns your entire team into tank-busters for 3 turns.

If the standard hit does ~800 damage to a high defense enemy, the extra 900 pushes it to ~1700. That is a "delete button" for nearly any non-tank hero.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Construct family is known for its "Core" mechanic, which rewards you for taking damage—a risky but rewarding playstyle for a squishy hero.
The family bonus isn't the main draw here, but pairing him with another Construct (like Brimstone or Sparklight) can provide small stat bumps.
This charges up as Frond takes damage. When he fires his special, the core detonates.
Sometimes it's worth letting Frond take a stray tile or slash attack to charge this core up before firing, turning him from a Hit-3 to a Hit-All nuker.
This is huge. He is immune to Mindless Attack, Silence, and Mana Block. He counters heroes like Miki, Peters, or the annoying silence from newer masked gods. He will

Upon release, Frond was a "nice to have" but not meta-breaking because boosted health wasn't on

were buffed to keep him relevant. 375% is higher than many modern Fast Hit-3s.
He is no longer a universal defense hero due to his squishiness. However, on

, he is a top-tier specialist. As long as the meta favors healers who boost HP (which it almost always does), Frond remains a premier counter. He is a "silver bullet" hero—you bring him to kill specific threats, not to brawl generally.
As of this review, Frond does not have a costume.
Frond is a Ranger. His primary job is to deal damage. Do not overthink this.
Maximizes the 375% multiplier. With his high base attack, every point of emblem attack scales significantly.

The Core mechanic (180% to all) can be useful for wave clearing if timed right.
Fire your Elemental Defense Down first, then regular Defense Down, then Frond. Even without boosted health, this combo will vaporize any three heroes it touches.
Use Frond specifically to target the enemy overhealer. Wait for them to fire (giving their team boosted HP), then punish them immediately. It sounds counter-intuitive to let them heal, but Frond
them to have that grey HP bar to trigger his massive 900 damage bonus.
Heroes who prevent buffs (since his +85% damage is a buff) can annoy him, but his main counters are just heavy hitters who kill him before he fires.
(Xnolphod, Ludwig) to ensure he fires repeatedly.

If you pulled Frond, rejoice! You have a solution to some of the most annoying teams in the game. Prioritize his Attack stats. Don't worry about his "low" defense; just hide him behind a taunter or pair him with a revive hero if you can. He will carry you through difficult raids against overhealers.

Frond is worth the tonic, but think twice about the Limit Break 2 (Alpha Aether). His Aether power (Regen) is weak, and his stats, even when 2LB, barely catch up to non-LB modern heroes. Use him as a surgical tool in wars. He doesn't need to be your strongest hero; he just needs to be strong enough to kill Hathor.
The community has had a rollercoaster relationship with Frond.
Initially mocked for his appearance ("I think he’s Rudolph’s cousin and they put him in the wrong portal").
Once players saw the math, the tone shifted. "The damage he does is insane... especially if enemies only have ‘low extra health’."
Players noted the massive improvements over time: "Ever since Frond got the buff... 2.9k to 3.1k damage each. Instant kill."
As one player put it, "He’s an upgraded version of Elkanen... If Elkanen actually did damage."

Frond is a relic of 2023 design—high utility, specific niche, but stats that require careful handling. He is not a "set and forget" defense hero. He is a scalpel. When you are facing a wall of boosted health that feels impossible to break, Frond is the one hero who turns that strength into a fatal weakness. He remains the premier "Boosted Health Assassin" in Empires & Puzzles.
Keep him, level him, but maybe buy him a helmet. He's fragile.

A deep dive into Frond, the Construct family's fast-mana sniper. We analyze his massive damage potential against boosted health, his recent buffs, and whether his aging stats still hold up in the 2026 meta.
